title: Turning On/Off
subtitle: Safe Switching Practice
overview: Students learn how to safely turn electrical devices ON and OFF using switches. They understand safe handling of batteries, plugs, wires, and practice proper switching habits through real-life activities.

images._note: Add photos from the actual classroom activity and safety demonstrations.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student switching ON classroom light
gallery.caption: Switch ON — Circuit Complete!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student switching OFF a lamp
gallery.caption: Switch OFF — Circuit Break!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Teacher showing correct plug handling
gallery.caption: Always hold the plug safely

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Demonstration of dry hands before touching switches
gallery.caption: Dry hands keep us safe

keyTopics: What happens when we turn ON a switch
What happens when we turn OFF a switch
Electricity flows only in a complete path
Safe handling of plugs and switches
Never touch switches with wet hands
Real-life switches — fan, TV, computer, torch

activity.title: ON/OFF Switch Practice
steps: Connect one wire from battery to LED
Turn switch OFF → LED stops glowing
Observe how electricity flows only when the path is complete

quiz.q: What happens when a switch is turned ON?
quiz.explanation: Switch ON means the circuit is complete, so electricity can flow.

quiz.q: What happens when a switch is turned OFF?
quiz.explanation: Switch OFF breaks the path, stopping electricity flow.

quiz.q: Should we touch switches with wet hands?
quiz.explanation: Wet hands can cause electric shock. Always keep hands dry.
Always hold the plug safely while removing it.

quiz.q: A complete electrical path is called:
quiz.explanation: A closed circuit allows electricity to flow.
Open circuit means the electrical path is incomplete.

quiz.q: Which device commonly uses an ON/OFF switch?
quiz.explanation: Fans use switches to control electricity flow.

quiz.q: What should you do if a wire is damaged?
quiz.explanation: Damaged wires are dangerous and should be reported immediately.

quiz.q: What glows when electricity flows in a simple circuit?
quiz.explanation: The LED or bulb converts electricity into light.
Switches help us safely control when electricity flows.
